---
title: "DataMining--Project"
author: "Bo"
date: "2026-03-24"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r cars}
# split dataset into training and testing
library(caTools)
# set up baseline regression: logistic regression. 
library(dplyr)
# both packages are for multinomial logistic regression
library(VGAM)
# package for neural network algorithm
library(nnet)
# Splitting data into training and testing
library(caTools)
# package for random forest algorithm 
library(randomForest)
# package for adaboosting algorithm
library(adabag)
# package for cross validation
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
#REF variable selection
library(caret)
# check correlation and plot it 
library(corrplot)
# for data manipulation
library(tidyverse)
# multicollinearity checking VIF
library(car)
# implement PCA. but since pca only work on numerical data, so we need another replacement that 
# can work on mixed datatype
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)

```


```{r}
numeric.data = read.csv("processed.csv", header = TRUE)
data = read.csv("processed.csv", header = TRUE)
```


```{r}
# Find column names where "?" exists
question.mark = names(data)[colSums(data == "?", na.rm = TRUE) > 0]
print(question.mark)
# to check ca and thal levels since there is special character in it. 
unique(data$ca)
unique(data$thal)
```

```{r}
# find out which row contains special characters
which(grepl("\\?", data$ca))
which(grepl("\\?", data$thal))
```

```{r}
removed.row = c(167, 193, 288, 303, 88, 267)
```


```{r}
numeric.data = numeric.data[-removed.row, ]
numeric.data$age = as.numeric(numeric.data$age)
numeric.data$sex = as.numeric(numeric.data$sex)
numeric.data$cp = as.numeric(numeric.data$cp)
numeric.data$trestbps = as.numeric(numeric.data$trestbps)
numeric.data$chol = as.numeric(numeric.data$chol)
numeric.data$fbs = as.numeric(numeric.data$fbs)
numeric.data$restecg = as.numeric(numeric.data$restecg)
numeric.data$thalach = as.numeric(numeric.data$thalach)
numeric.data$exang = as.numeric(numeric.data$exang)
numeric.data$oldpeak = as.numeric(numeric.data$oldpeak)
numeric.data$slope = as.numeric(numeric.data$slope)
numeric.data$ca = as.numeric(numeric.data$ca)
numeric.data$thal = as.numeric(numeric.data$thal)
```


```{r}
correlations=cor(numeric.data[,-14])
corrplot(correlations, method="number", number.cex= 8/ncol(numeric.data))

```


```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
data$num = ifelse(data$num == 0, 0, 1)
data$ca = as.factor(data$ca)
data$thal = as.factor(data$thal)
data$num = as.factor(data$num)
data$sex = as.factor(data$sex)
data$cp = as.factor(data$cp)
data$fbs = as.factor(data$fbs)
data$restecg = as.factor(data$restecg)
data$fbs = as.factor(data$fbs)
data$exang = as.factor(data$exang)
data$slope = as.factor(data$slope)
```


```{r}
file = data[-removed.row, ]
dim(file)
```


```{r}
# since the unit of each column is different, they need a normalization.
normalization = function(x){
  z = (x-mean(x))/(sd(x))
}
```



```{r}
file[, c("age", "trestbps", "chol", "thalach", "oldpeak")] = as.data.frame(lapply(file[, c("age", "trestbps", "chol", "thalach", "oldpeak")], normalization))
```


```{r}
split = sample.split(file, SplitRatio = 0.8)
train = subset(file, split == "TRUE")
test = subset(file, split == "FALSE")
dim(train)
```

```{r}
train%>%ggplot(aes(num)) + geom_bar() + labs(x="Num", y="Absolute Frequency")
```



```{r}
#multinomial logistic regression using vgam package
logistic = glm(num~., data=train, family = "binomial")
summary(logistic)
logistic.pred = predict(logistic, newdata=test, type="response")
logis.pred = ifelse(logistic.pred >= 0.5, 1, 0)
logis.confusion = confusionMatrix(test$num, as.factor(logis.pred))
logis.accuracy = logis.confusion$overall[1]
logis.accuracy
```

```{r}
# Multicollinearity checking, here need to use car:: even car is loaded by library()
vif.result = car::vif(logistic)
vif.result
```


```{r}
# FAMD is same like pca,but famd can work on mixed datatype, pca only works on numerical data
famd = FAMD(train, sup.var = 14, graph= FALSE, ncp=25)
get_eigenvalue(famd)
```


```{r}
# check outliers
cooks.distance(logistic)[which.max(cooks.distance(logistic))]
```


```{r}
plot(logistic, which = 4)
```





```{r}
random = randomForest(num~.,data=train, importance=TRUE, mtry = 10, ntree=500)

varImpPlot(random)

random.pred = predict(random, test, type="class")

random.confusion = confusionMatrix(as.factor(random.pred), test$num)
random.confusion

```


```{r}
# random forest with 10 folds cv
repeat_cv = trainControl(method='repeatedcv', number=10, repeats=3)
tenfold.forest = train(num~., data=train, method='rf', trControl=repeat_cv, metric='Accuracy')
tenfold.forest
tenfold.forest.pred = predict(tenfold.forest, newdata=test)
forest.confusion = table(tenfold.forest.pred, test$num )
forest.accuracy = sum(diag(forest.confusion))/sum(forest.confusion)
forest.accuracy
```



```{r}
# ada boosting 
ada.boost = boosting(num~., data = train, mfinal = 10, boos = TRUE)
ada.pred = predict(ada.boost, newdata= test)
ada.confusion= table(ada.pred$class, test$num)
ada.accuracy = sum(diag(ada.confusion)) / sum(ada.confusion)
ada.accuracy
```



```{r}
# Specify RFE control parameters with naive Bayes function
naive.ctrl = suppressWarnings(rfeControl(functions = nbFuncs, method = "cv", number = 10) ) 

# Perform RFE
naive.rfe = suppressWarnings(rfe(file[, -14], file[, 14], sizes = c(1:13), rfeControl = naive.ctrl) ) 

# Print RFE results
print(naive.rfe)
```


```{r}
# Specify RFE control parameters with random forest function
forest.ctrl = suppressWarnings(rfeControl(functions = rfFuncs, method = "cv", number = 10) ) 
# Perform RFE
forest.rfe = suppressWarnings(rfe(file[, -14], file[, 14], sizes = c(1:13), rfeControl = forest.ctrl) ) 
# Print RFE results
print(forest.rfe)
```



```{r}
# Specify RFE control parameters with logistic regression function
logistic.ctrl = suppressWarnings(rfeControl(functions = lrFuncs, method = "cv", number = 10) ) 

# Perform RFE
logistic.rfe = suppressWarnings(rfe(num~., data=train, sizes = c(1:13), rfeControl=logistic.ctrl) )

# Print RFE results
print(logistic.rfe)
```



```{r}
# linear discriminant analysis on RFE
lda.ctrl = suppressWarnings(rfeControl(functions = ldaFuncs, method = "cv", number = 10) ) 
# Perform RFE
lda.rfe = suppressWarnings(rfe(num~., data=train, sizes = c(1:13), rfeControl=lda.ctrl) )
# Print RFE results
print(lda.rfe)
```



```{r}
# Specify RFE control parameters with bagged  trees function
treebag.ctrl = suppressWarnings(rfeControl(functions = treebagFuncs, method = "cv", number = 10) ) 

# Perform RFE
treebag.rfe = suppressWarnings(rfe(file[, -14], file[, 14], sizes = c(1:13), rfeControl = treebag.ctrl) ) 

# Print RFE results
print(treebag.rfe)
```


```{r}
logistic.two = glm(num~slope+cp+thal+ca, data=train, family = "binomial")
logistic.pred.two = predict(logistic.two, newdata=test, type="response")
logis.pred.two = ifelse(logistic.pred.two >= 0.5, 1, 0)
logis.confusion.two = confusionMatrix(test$num, as.factor(logis.pred.two))
logis.accuracy.two = logis.confusion.two$overall[1]
logis.accuracy.two
```


```{r}
repeat_cv = trainControl(method='repeatedcv', number=10, repeats=3)

tenfold.forest.two = train(num~thal+ca+cp+oldpeak+sex, data=train, method='rf', trControl=repeat_cv, metric='Accuracy')

tenfold.forest.pred.two = predict(tenfold.forest.two, newdata=test)

forest.confusion.two = table(tenfold.forest.pred.two, test$num )

forest.accuracy.two = sum(diag(forest.confusion.two))/sum(forest.confusion.two)
forest.accuracy.two
```


```{r}
# ada boosting 
ada.boost.two = boosting(num~thal+ca+cp+thalach+oldpeak, data = train, mfinal = 10, boos = TRUE)
ada.pred.two = predict(ada.boost.two, newdata= test)

ada.confusion.two= table(ada.pred.two$class, test$num)
ada.accuracy.two = sum(diag(ada.confusion.two)) / sum(ada.confusion.two)
ada.accuracy.two
```
